journal_entry.description: Purchase of merchandise inventory and related costs
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS (IAS 2), inventory cost includes purchase price plus costs necessary to bring inventory to its present location and condition. Freight to warehouse ($120,000) and shipping insurance ($30,000) are capitalizable as they are incurred before the inventory is ready for sale. Post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) and outbound delivery freight ($50,000) are period costs expensed as incurred because they occur after inventory is ready for sale. HST at 13% is recoverable on all purchases.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Merchandise purchase on account increases inventory (asset) and accounts payable (liability). Freight to warehouse and shipping insurance are capitalized to inventory per IAS 2. Post-acquisition storage and outbound freight are operating expenses. All cash payments reduce cash. HST at 13% (ON) is recoverable on all purchases. Debit tuple: 2 asset increases (inventory entries) + 2 expense increases (storage + freight out) = [2,0,2,0,0,0]. Credit tuple: 2 liability increases (AP + HST payable conceptually, but here AP for merchandise+inbound costs, and cash reduction) + 1 asset decrease (cash) = [2,0,0,1,0,0].
